findings.summary: Top-level sheet correctly parsed as hierarchy-only with 4 sub-sheets; aggregate component statistics match sum across sub-sheets

correct.description: The top-level sheet contains only sheet references (wireless_module, can_module, rs485module, power_module) and no components of its own. The analyzer correctly reports total_components=131 (wireless 63 + CAN 14 + RS485 38 + power 16) and aggregates all BOM, component, and net data across sub-sheets. All 131 component references are accounted for. The 135 entries in the components array vs 131 in statistics is correct because U202 (4066 quad analog switch) contributes 5 entries (one per unit) that are deduplicated in the count.

findings.summary: CAN bus correctly detected with ISO1050DUB (U401) and TJA1051T (U402) on CAN_H/CAN_L nets; component references and pin mappings are accurate; Isolation barrier detected GND/ISO_GND domains but iso...

correct.description: The analyzer correctly identifies all 14 components (R401, JP401, JP402, J401, U401, R403, U402, J402, C401, D401, R402, C402, C403, C404). CAN_H and CAN_L nets are correctly populated with both transceivers. The bus_analysis.can section correctly lists both CAN_H and CAN_L nets with U401 and U402 as devices. The ERC warning correctly flags RX-CAN having two output drivers (U401 pin RXD and U402 pin RXD in parallel). Power domains correctly attribute +3.3V to U401 (VCC1) and U402 (VCC), and ISO_5V to U401 (VCC2). The SM712 TVS diode (D401) on CAN bus lines is correctly classified as a diode.

incorrect.description: The isolation_barriers entry correctly identifies GND and ISO_GND as separate ground domains, but isolation_components is empty and isolated_power_rails is empty. U401 (ISO1050DUB) is the component providing galvanic isolation between the two domains — it has VCC1/GND1 on the 3.3V side and VCC2/GND2 (ISO_GND) on the isolated CAN bus side. The analyzer found the isolation structure but failed to attribute the isolator component. Additionally, ground_domains.multiple_domains is reported as false despite clearly having two distinct ground domains (GND and ISO_GND), and cross_domain_signals is empty when TX-CAN and RX-CAN traverse the isolation boundary inside U401.

findings.summary: RS485 module components correctly parsed; MAX485E (U301, U303) detected in RS485 bus analysis with correct A/B/DI/RO/DE/RE net mapping; U302 (MAX3486) RS485 transceiver missing from rs485 bus analy...

correct.description: All 38 components are correctly identified including 3 RS485 ICs (U301/U302/U303), 3 RJ45 connectors, 3 TVS diodes (SM712), 3 2-pin connectors, 18×10K resistors, 3×120R termination resistors, 4×0.1uF capacitors, and 1 solder jumper. U301 and U303 (MAX485E) are correctly detected as RS485 transceivers with proper A/B differential pair nets and control signals. The ERC warnings for RX-485 multi-driver and no-driver nets on RS485_VCC, RE-485, TX-485, and DE-485 are accurate — these signals are driven from the ESP32 sub-sheet via global labels. BOM correctly groups 18 resistors as 10K and 3 as 120R.

missed.description: The design has three RS485 transceivers: U301 and U303 (MAX485E) plus U302 (MAX3486). The analyzer's RS485 detector found U301 and U303 but missed U302. U302 has identical pin function to MAX485E (pins 1=RO, 2=~RE, 3=DE, 4=DI, 6=A, 7=B, 8=VCC) and is connected to the same RS485 bus via nets A1/B1 for its differential pair. The likely cause is that the detector pattern-matches on the value or lib_id and covers 'MAX485' but not 'MAX3486'. Both share the Interface_UART library. This should be a third rs485 entry with transceiver='U302', a_net='A1', b_net='B1'.

findings.summary: Power module correctly identifies AMS1117-3.3 LDO (U501), power rails, BOM, and 16 component references; Isolation barrier detected for power module (GND/ISO_GND) but isolation_components is empty ...

correct.description: All 16 component references are correctly parsed: C501-C506, D501, FL501, J501, J502, PS501, PS502, R501, RV501, TH501, U501. The AMS1117-3.3 (U501) is correctly identified as an LDO regulator with +5V input and +3V3 output. The RV501 (varistor 10D561) is correctly classified as a varistor and listed in protection_devices. The isolated DC/DC converter PS501 (B0505S-1WR3, Mornsun) correctly outputs ISO_5V/ISO_GND as single_pin_nets (not connected to anything else in this sub-sheet — they connect via global labels to the CAN module). The HLK-5M05 AC/DC module PS502 is correctly parsed with its MPN. EMI filter FL501 (10mH) is correctly typed as 'filter'. The power supply tree (+5V from HLK-5M05 → AMS1117 → +3V3) is correctly captured.

incorrect.description: The power_module.kicad_sch contains PS501 (B0505S-1WR3), a Mornsun 5V-to-5V isolated DC/DC converter that provides the isolated power rail (ISO_5V/ISO_GND) for the CAN bus side. The isolation_barriers detector correctly identifies the GND and ISO_GND ground domains but lists isolation_components as empty. PS501 is the isolating component (its input pins connect to GND/+5V, its output pins connect to ISO_GND/ISO_5V). This is the same missed-detection pattern as in the CAN module.

missed.description: The power_regulators section only contains U501 (AMS1117-3.3 LDO). PS502 (HLK-5M05) is a 220VAC-to-5VDC isolated switching power supply module and PS501 (B0505S-1WR3) is a 5V-in, 5V-out isolated DC/DC converter — neither is detected in power_regulators or any other signal_analysis section. These are major power conversion elements in the design. The design_observations also incorrectly flags U501 as missing decoupling capacitors on +5V and +3V3 rails when C501/C503 are present on +5V and C504/C505/C506 are on +3V3 — this is likely a net-topology traversal issue where the capacitors are not attributed to the correct rail.

findings.summary: ESP32-S3-MINI module (U201), 34 test points, SPI bus, UART buses, USB differential pair, I2C, transistors, switches, and SD card connector all correctly parsed; SD card (J205 Micro_SD_Card) not det...

correct.description: All 63 unique components are correctly identified. U201 (ESP32-S3-MINI-S3-WROOM-1) is correctly typed as IC. The 34 test points (TP201-TP234) are correctly typed and listed. SPI bus is detected with MISO/MOSI/SCK nets all attributed to U201, with chip_select_count=1 (SD_CS). UART signals UART0TX/UART0RX and UART1TX/UART1RX correctly attributed to U201. I2C SDA/SCL correctly detected on U201. USB differential pair (USB+/USB-) correctly detected with ESD protection via U201. The two NPN transistors Q201/Q202 (MMBT3904) are correctly typed. JP203-JP206 (SolderJumper_3_Open, in_bom=False) are correctly excluded from missing_mpn. J201 (Socket_01x02) has mpn='?' which is a placeholder non-empty value and is correctly excluded from missing_mpn. Global labels TX-485/RX-485/TX-CAN/RX-CAN are correctly captured for cross-sheet connectivity.

incorrect.description: The I2C bus analysis reports SDA and SCL with has_pull_up=false and empty pull_ups arrays. Examining the wireless_module schematic confirms there are no pull-up resistors on SDA or SCL in this sheet — U201 (ESP32-S3-MINI) is the only device on these nets, and the pull-ups are not present anywhere in any of the four sub-sheets analyzed. This means the I2C bus genuinely lacks pull-up resistors across the entire design. The observation is factually correct and not a false positive, but it is worth confirming: SDA/SCL are global labels that could connect to pull-ups on a main PCB not captured in these schematics. Within the scope of these files the warning is accurate.

The power_domains.ic_power_rails for U202 (4066) lists '__unnamed_5' as the power rail, indicating that the VDD/VSS supply pins of the 4066 are connected to wires without net labels. This represents a real schematic gap (floating/unlabeled power supply) that the analyzer correctly flags by showing an unnamed net. The design_analysis.power_domains.domain_groups also groups U202 under '__unnamed_5'. This is a genuine schematic quality issue in the source file, not an analyzer error — the 4066's supply pins are not connected to named power rails like +3V3 or +5V.

missed.description: J205 is a Micro_SD_Card socket connected to U201's SPI bus (MOSI, MISO, SCK, SD_CS). The memory_interfaces list is empty. J205 is explicitly a Micro_SD_Card type but the analyzer does not add it to memory_interfaces. The SPI bus analysis correctly identifies the SD_CS chip select (count=1) but does not name J205 as the associated SPI device. Additionally, J205 is listed as missing_footprint (empty footprint field in schematic) which is a legitimate observation but the missing memory interface is the primary gap.

findings.summary: I2C bus_analysis and design_observations disagree on pullup presence; isolation_barriers detects ISO_GND domain but lists no isolation_components; PS501 and PS502 classified as 'connector' instead ...

correct.description: U501 (AMS1117-3.3) correctly identified as LDO topology with input_rail='+5V' and output_rail='+3V3', vout=3.3V via fixed_suffix heuristic.
Both MMBT3904 transistors detected with correct base/collector/emitter net assignments (EN, RTS, DTR, RST signals), base resistors included.
USB+ and USB- correctly identified as differential pair with has_esd:true attributed to U201 (ESP32-S3-MINI which has integrated USB PHY with ESD).

RX-485 (3 MAX485/MAX3486 RO outputs tied together) and RX-CAN (ISO1050DUB + TJA1051T RXD tied together) both correctly flagged as output conflicts. These are real topology concerns even if intentional.

incorrect.description: design_observations correctly reports has_pullup:true for SDA/SCL with R204/R205 (0R_0603 resistors each with one pin on +3V3 and other on SDA/SCL). Simultaneously, bus_analysis.i2c reports has_pull_up:false and pull_ups:[] for both SDA and SCL. The bus_analysis subsystem fails to detect these pullups. Both subsystems should agree.

The analyzer correctly finds two ground domains (GND and ISO_GND) and reports an isolation barrier entry. However, isolation_components is empty — U401 (ISO1050DUB, 'Isolated CAN Transceiver') is not identified as the isolation component despite its lib_id being 'Interface_CAN_LIN:ISO1050DUB' and its description explicitly stating 'Isolated'. PS501 (B0505S-1WR3, isolated 5V DC/DC) is also missed.

PS501 (B0505S-1WR3, 5V-to-5V isolated DC/DC converter) and PS502 (HLK-5M05, AC/DC mains-to-5V module) are both classified as type='connector'. Neither appears in power_regulators. These are power supply modules and should be classified accordingly, or at minimum detected as regulators/power modules.

findings.summary: Same I2C bus_analysis vs design_observations pullup inconsistency present in second file; Same ISO1050DUB isolation_components miss in second file

incorrect.description: The second design (ESP32-WROOM-32E variant, U203) exhibits identical behavior: design_observations reports has_pullup:true for R204/R205, bus_analysis reports has_pull_up:false. Both files share the same hardware base so this confirms the bug is systemic in the bus_analysis subsystem.

File 2 also has ISO_GND ground domain and U401 ISO1050DUB present, and also reports isolation_components:[] and isolated_power_rails:[]. Confirms the isolation component identification gap is systemic.
